featureName: BUG-021-010: hospitality guest/property alerts LLM-driven on a reusable judgment foundation
reporter: owner directive (no const limits; LLM decides) + best-solution-long-term-but-short-term-value

executionHistory.summary: Two findings. (1) The GuestHost digest's guest/property concern alerts were decided by hardcoded SQL thresholds (queryGuestAlerts: sentiment_score < 0.3, total_stays > 1; queryPropertyAlerts: avg_rating < 3.5, issue_count >= 5) — the same docs/smackerel.md §3.6 domain reasoning the prior four conversions moved to the LLM, but the digest package had NO bridge access. (2) The four prior agent.Bridge evaluators (cooling/alert-timing/resurface/expertise) each re-implemented the identical marshal→invoke→validate→decode plumbing — duplication the owner's 'best solution long term' directive calls to converge. Decision: extract agent.InvokeJudgment[T] (long-term foundation) and ship hospitality on it (short-term value), the first digest-package LLM judgment.

Single bugfix-fastlane scope: (a) agent.InvokeJudgment[T] + JudgmentRunner + ErrJudgmentUnavailable in the agent package (natural home; depends only on IntentEnvelope/InvocationResult/OutcomeOK; no cycle); (b) one batched hospitality_concern_evaluate scenario (guests + properties in one call — the digest runs daily, capped); (c) BridgeHospitalityEvaluator on the foundation; (d) gather-signals + LLM-judge rework; (e) Bridge wiring into the digest generator + scenario-lint blank import for BS-010; (f) operational candidate caps to fail-loud SST. Migrating the existing four evaluators is a deferred behaviour-preserving follow-up (out of scope to keep blast radius on the new behaviour). SCN-021-HOSP-01..03 authored.

Created internal/agent/judgment.go (InvokeJudgment[T], JudgmentRunner, ErrJudgmentUnavailable). config/prompt_contracts/hospitality-concern-evaluate-v1.yaml. internal/digest/hospitality_eval.go: GuestSignal/PropertySignal (Email json:"-", Ref correlation), ConcernJudgment, HospitalityDecision, HospitalityEvaluator + BridgeHospitalityEvaluator (on InvokeJudgment), HospitalityBounds, noop_hospitality_concern init(). Reworked hospitality.go: gatherGuestSignals/gatherPropertySignals replace the threshold queries; assembleConcernAlerts runs the LLM and maps by ref; AssembleHospitalityContext takes eval + bounds. generator.go gained hospitalityEval/hospitalityBounds + SetHospitalityEvaluator + the call-site pass-through. SST: digest.hospitality.* (smackerel.yaml + config.sh + internal/config/hospitality.go). Wiring: cmd/core/wiring_hospitality.go + main.go call; cmd/scenario-lint blank-imports internal/digest.

agent/judgment_test.go: routing, source, signal forwarding, json:"-" non-leak, nil-runner sentinel, all error paths. digest/hospitality_eval_test.go: batch parse, routing, guest/property forwarding, internal-Email non-leak, empty-input short-circuit, all error paths. config/hospitality_test.go: SST populate + fail-loud + range. scenario-lint registers hospitality_concern_evaluate (14, up from 13); go build ./... + vet green.

Full agent + digest + config + cmd/core + scenario-lint packages green. The pre-existing 16 hospitality unit tests (IsEmpty_*, FormatHospitalityFallback_*, DigestContext_With/WithoutHospitality, IsGuestHostActive_NilRegistry) stay green — they assert struct/format/empty semantics, not the removed threshold queries. ./smackerel.sh config generate resolves the new keys into dev.env (192-193).

The new foundation NET-REMOVES future duplication: hospitality's evaluator is ~6 lines on InvokeJudgment instead of the ~30-line marshal/invoke/validate/decode block the prior four carry. One batched scenario + one evaluator + one assembleConcernAlerts mapping. The two threshold query functions collapse into two signal-gather functions + one LLM mapping.

assembleConcernAlerts gathers all candidate signals before the single LLM call; out-of-range refs from the model are guarded and skipped with a warning; empty candidate sets short-circuit without an LLM call; nil evaluator / unavailable judgment / parse error all degrade to no concern alerts (the rest of the digest is unaffected). No DB cursor held during Invoke.

GuestSignal.Email is json:"-" and proven (by test) not to enter the LLM envelope; only name + counts + sentiment/rating are sent (names already appear in the LLM-generated digest). The signal queries are parameterized (LIMIT $1). The foundation's InvokeJudgment performs no logging of payloads. No framework files touched.
artifact-lint PASSED for the packet; config generate + build + vet + affected packages green. Evidence in report.md.

No hardcoded hospitality alert threshold remains (grep of sentiment_score < 0.3 | avg_rating < 3.5 | issue_count >= 5 in hospitality.go = 0). No migration; generated env gitignored; no .github/bubbles changes; the AssembleHospitalityContext signature change is contained (only generator.go calls it, confirmed repo-wide); parent spec 021 + spec 013 planning artifacts untouched.

judgment.go, hospitality_eval.go, hospitality.go, smackerel.yaml, and the wiring document the reusable foundation, the LLM-driven judgment, and the operational/business boundary. The packet records continuation of BUG-021-009, the owner's best-long-term-plus-short-term-value framing, and the deferred migration of the existing four evaluators onto the foundation.

scopeProgress.title: Reusable judgment foundation + LLM-driven hospitality alerts

lockdownState.reason: bugfix-fastlane LLM-driven rework; no convergence loop required

resolvedFindings.summary: The GuestHost digest decided guest/property concern alerts with hardcoded SQL thresholds (sentiment_score < 0.3, avg_rating < 3.5, issue_count >= 5, total_stays > 1), contradicting docs/smackerel.md §3.6 and the owner directive; the digest package had no LLM access.
resolvedFindings.resolution: Introduced agent.InvokeJudgment[T] and routed the hospitality concern decision through the hospitality_concern_evaluate scenario via BridgeHospitalityEvaluator; the digest gathers candidate signals within operational caps only; caps moved to fail-loud SST. No hardcoded threshold remains; nil evaluator ⇒ no concern alerts.
resolvedFindings.evidence: report.md Test/Validation/Audit Evidence; internal/agent/judgment.go; internal/digest/hospitality_eval.go; grep of the three thresholds in hospitality.go = 0.

resolvedFindings.summary: The four prior agent.Bridge judgment evaluators each re-implemented the identical marshal→invoke→validate→decode plumbing — duplication the owner's best-solution-long-term directive calls to converge.
resolvedFindings.resolution: Extracted agent.InvokeJudgment[T] (+ JudgmentRunner + ErrJudgmentUnavailable) as the single reusable primitive, unit-tested, and made hospitality its first native consumer. Migrating the existing four onto it is a deferred behaviour-preserving follow-up guarded by their unit tests.
resolvedFindings.evidence: report.md Test Evidence (TestInvokeJudgment_*); internal/agent/judgment.go; design.md Alternatives Considered.
